primary_naics.description: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services

secondary_naics.relationship: Adjacent/overlapping code frequently used for cybersecurity consulting, managed security services (MSSP), and incident response — many OT/ICS security boutiques (and Accenture Security engagements) are classified here.
Adjacent service line — covers outsourced management of client IT/OT systems and data processing facilities, relevant to Accenture's managed security operations work.

secondary_naics.description: Other Scientific and Technical Consulting Services
secondary_naics.relationship: Adjacent advisory service — used by firms delivering OT/ICS risk, safety, and technical security consulting outside strict IT classification.
Adjacent/competitor overlap — engineering firms (e.g., process control/automation engineering houses) that design and secure industrial control systems, a core OT cybersecurity client base.
Closely related code under which Accenture Federal Services is also classified; covers custom secure software/firmware development for OT environments.
Adjacent physical/electronic security services provider category, sometimes bundled with OT/ICS security integration for industrial facilities.

secondary_naics.description: Instruments and Related Products Manufacturing for Measuring, Displaying, and Controlling Industrial Process Variables
secondary_naics.relationship: Manufacturer served — makers of SCADA/DCS/PLC hardware (e.g., Honeywell, Emerson, Yokogawa) whose products Accenture's OT security practice must assess, harden, and integrate.
Manufacturer served — industrial control/relay equipment vendors (relevant to grid, manufacturing, and utility OT environments) that are clients or system suppliers in OT security engagements.
Manufacturer served — includes industrial networking/communications gear used in ICS/SCADA architectures secured by OT cybersecurity teams.
Key client/end-market sector — electric utilities are a primary target industry for OT cybersecurity consulting due to critical infrastructure regulation (NERC CIP).
Key client/end-market sector — oil & gas refining relies heavily on ICS/SCADA and is a major buyer of OT cybersecurity advisory services.

secondary_naics.description: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
secondary_naics.relationship: Adjacent/competitor — cloud and hosting providers whose infrastructure increasingly converges with OT environments (IT/OT convergence), also a common classification for cybersecurity software/services firms.

naics_rationale: Accenture is classified across several overlapping NAICS codes depending on the reporting source: business-database aggregators most often list it under 541618 (Other Management Consulting Services), while ESG/industry profiles and Accenture Federal Services government contracting records use 541511 (Custom Computer Programming Services) and 541611 (Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services). Because OT/ICS cybersecurity consulting sits at the intersection of technology integration and management advisory, 541512 (Computer Systems Design Services) is the most broadly cited primary code for cybersecurity consulting generally, since it is explicitly the code most cybersecurity firms self-select for consulting, integration, and advisory work. Secondary codes capture (1) closely adjacent IT/cyber service lines Accenture and its competitors (Deloitte, IBM, Booz Allen, Dragos, Claroty, Nozomi Networks) also use — 541519, 541513, 541690, 541511, and 518210; (2) physical/industrial security overlap (561621); and (3) the manufacturer/end-client base that defines the OT cybersecurity target market — industrial control and instrumentation manufacturers (334513, 335314, 334290) and critical-infrastructure operators such as electric utilities (221122) and oil & gas refiners (324110), which are the principal buyers of OT security advisory services and thus valuable for mapping target employers and clients in a job search.
